simple_substance.name: Simple substance
simple_substance.rule: The oxidation state of an element in a simple substance = 0

fluorine.name: Fluorine
fluorine.rule: F always has an oxidation state of −1 (the most electronegative element)

group1.name: Alkali metals (Group I)
group1.rule: Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr — always +1

group2.name: Alkaline earth metals (Group II)
group2.rule: Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra — always +2

oxygen.name: Oxygen
oxygen.rule: O is usually −2. Exception: in peroxides (H₂O₂, Na₂O₂) → −1

hydrogen.name: Hydrogen
hydrogen.rule: H is usually +1. Exception: in metal hydrides (NaH, CaH₂) → −1

algebraic.name: Algebraic calculation
algebraic.rule: The sum of oxidation states of all atoms in a neutral molecule = 0. Knowing all OS except one, solve the equation.

_redox.oxidizer: An oxidizing agent is an atom (element) that accepts electrons. Its oxidation state decreases.
_redox.reducer: A reducing agent is an atom (element) that donates electrons. Its oxidation state increases.
_redox.mnemonic: LEO the lion says GER: Lose Electrons → Oxidation, Gain Electrons → Reduction.
